perturbation.original_text: After complete of the project construction, Party A shall be responsible for the operation, maintenance and management of the recycling project. Party A shall charge Party B energy saving service fee according to the income from CDQ waste heat power generation station.
perturbation.changed_text: After completion of the project construction, Party A shall be responsible for the operation, maintenance, and management of the recycling project.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'Party A shall charge Party B energy saving service fee according to the income from CDQ waste heat power generation station', the modified text omits the key method of compensation for Party A. This creates an in-text contradiction because Item Three then discusses the calculation and payment of this fee, but the basis for the fee (income from CDQ waste heat power generation) is now undefined, leading to ambiguity in how Party A will be compensated and creating an enforcement challenge.
perturbation.location: Item Two, Operation of Recycling Project, Section 1
perturbation.contradicted_location: Item Three, Cost and Operation Target
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party B shall pay Party A energy saving service fee after the construction of CDQ system and CDQ waste heat power generation system are completed (upon passing evaluation of the ability of connection to the grid for power generation). 1. The energy saving service fee is paid in the form of electricity fee. The calculation basis of energy saving service fee is as follows: average operational time is 8,000 hours per annually; if the annual average operational time was less than 8,000 hours due to the reasons of Party B, the operational time is calculated as 8,000 hours per year; if the annual average operational time was less than 8,000 hours due to the reasons by Party A, the operational time is calculated based on the actual operational hours. 2. If the grid-connected electricity price is adjusted since the execution of this agreement, the fee shall be calculated as the energy saving service fee (stipulated in section 1 of Item III ) plus 85% of the price change/adjustment from the original grid-connected electricity price. The starting day for the change will be from the date of adjustment by Shandong Provincial Price Department. 3. The amount of power generated shall be calculated based on the indicator of electricity meter in the power station. 4. The settlement date is the first day of the next month. Deducting the dues of Party A, Party B should pay Party A the energy saving service fee of the last month before the 15t h of each month. 5. Payment method: Wire or transfer. 6. The policy rebate, award, and preferential treatment shall be shared by both Parties. Each party shares 50% of the policy rebate, award, and preferential treatment. Both Parties share the expenses incurred in the process of the application for the policy rebate, award, and preferential treatment.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that Party A will charge Party B a fee based on income from the CDQ waste heat power generation station. The changed text removes this payment condition. The contradicted text details the calculation and payment schedule of this energy saving service fee. Because the changed text removes the basis for the energy saving service fee, it directly contradicts the contradicted text, making the calculation and payment details in Item Three unenforceable due to lack of a defined payment trigger/condition.

perturbation.original_text: The construction period of the project is 12 months from the execution of technology agreement. If the project was delayed due to Party A, Party B has right to dispose the equipment on the construction site after two month past due date.
perturbation.changed_text: The construction period of the project is 12 months from the execution of technology agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'If the project was delayed due to Party A, Party B has right to dispose the equipment on the construction site after two month past due date', the modified text removes Party B's recourse in the event of a significant delay caused by Party A. This creates an in-text contradiction because Item Thirteen, Liability for Breach of Agreements, refers to consequences for failing to complete the project on schedule, but it is not clear how the dispose of equipment is being reffered within that breach, now making the breach unclear on how to enforce it.
perturbation.location: Item Five Construction and Construction Period of CDQ System and CDQ waste heat power generation system, Section 1
perturbation.contradicted_location: Item Thirteen Liability for Breach of Agreements, Section 3
perturbation.contradicted_text: If any event affects the ability to its continue operation of the Party A or Party B, such as bankruptcy, going out of business, merging, transferring, separation or being dissolution, such party must give the other party a written notice within 30 days and provide documentary evidences. If such party cannot perform the contractual obligation, the other party suffered from loss could claim for compensation.
perturbation.justification: The original text gave Party B the right to dispose of equipment if Party A delayed the project. The changed text removes this right. The contradicted text states that if a party cannot perform the contractual obligation, the other party can claim compensation. By removing Party B's right to dispose of equipment as recourse for Party A's delay, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text because it removes a specific remedy for a breach, making it unclear how the 'loss' suffered can be addressed, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: During the contact period, Party A has the ownership of the CDQ and CDQ waste heat power generation systems. After the termination of the contact, Party B has the disposition right to the project.
perturbation.changed_text: During the contact period, Party A has the ownership of the CDQ and CDQ waste heat power generation systems.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'After the termination of the contact, Party B has the disposition right to the project', the modified text removes Party B's right to the project after contract termination. This contradicts section 4 of Item Sixteen, which states 'When the agreement is terminated, Party A has rights to dispose all assets of the recycling project'. While this section gives Party A the right to dispose of the assets, it is now contradictory since they would need Party B's go ahead since it is not said that Party B has the right to dispose of the project upon termination.
perturbation.location: Item Eight Ownership and Intellectual Property of the Recycling Project, Section 1
perturbation.contradicted_location: Item Sixteen Agreement, Appendix, and Others, Section 4
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall be signed and sealed by legal representatives or authorized representatives of both parties and take effect from the effective date. 2. After the agreement is signed, Party A shall complete its due diligence and provide Party B with the letter of confirmation. Parties shall sign Technical Appendix within 90 days after the agreement is signed. 3. The Technical Appendix and Performance Guarantee are an integral part of the agreement and have the same legal effect of the agreement. 4. The agreement can only be terminated after negotiation and agreement by Party A and B in writing. When the agreement is terminated, Party A has rights to dispose all assets of the recycling project. 5. As for matters not mentioned herein, Party A and Party B shall sign a supplemental agreement through negotiation. The supplemental agreement has same effect to the agreement. If there is any conflicts, the latest supplemental agreement prevails. The agreement is made in quadruplicate. Each party holds two copies and they have the same legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text gave Party B the 'disposition right to the project' after termination. The changed text removes this right. The contradicted text states 'When the agreement is terminated, Party A has rights to dispose all assets of the recycling project.' Without Party B's disposition right as stated in the original contract, Party A disposing of all recycling project assets after termination directly conflicts with Party B previously having disposition rights. This creates a contradiction in obligations. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.
